calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Continued To Jan-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robin Klomparens appeared by telephone for Richard Kopacz.
Jack Fergusom for Marie Kausak (Objector).

The Court stated that issues were raised as far as the expenses that were incurred by the Personal Representative and when they were incurred, that it has concerns, and that it is not clear as to why four family members had to be flown out here to do the work and why they had to fly first class. The Court also stated that there are questions of expenses incurred prior to appointment, whether it is appropriate for the estate to bear the cost of the two week stay and questions whether there is any personal property that hasn't been preserved for distribution. Ms. Klomparens stated that the response laid out the facts, that the Decedent was a hoarder, that she instructed the parties not to throw anything away, that it wasn't a vacation for them and that they stayed in a modest MLS. Ms. Klomparens also stated that they hauled back over 30 boxes, that the Decedent did not have any sentimental items except pictures and that she wasn't aware that they all flew first class. In addition, Ms. Klomparens stated that airfare of $722.00 is not first class, that it was $1,400.00 for two flights, and that they had to deal with the body, the death certificate and the landlord. Mr. Ferguson stated that the flights were booked on a Thursday, that they flew out on Saturday, that they went back on first class, that it is not appropriate, and that they don't know what was kept and what was thrown away. Mr. Ferguson also stated that he wants Counsel to tell them what they kept, that he does not think the estate should pay for the MLS for the Personal Representative's family, that if the Personal Representative couldn't do the work he shouldn't have been appointed and that his Client has never spoken with the Personal Representative in over seven years. In addition, Mr. Ferguson stated that there was no notice of proposed action, that they still don't know what was in those boxes, that it costed $4,000.00 to ship the boxes and spoke of the cost of the U-Haul and gas. Ms. Klomparens stated that the U-Haul was the most economical way, that they had to work quickly, that no one else volunteered, that she has spoken with Mr. Ferguson's Client, that she understands that the personal property were just photos and that they can distribute them anyway the Court wants them to. Mr. Ferguson stated that $17,292.00 in stock were sold in 2018, that it does not appear in the accounting and it is not in losses, and that he has requested records of the PG&E stock sale. Ms. Klomparens stated that she thinks it's in the cash and she thinks this issue came up before, that she can address it, that she is certain there is an explanation for it and that she can submit something on that issue. The Court asked that the Personal Representative submit something addressing the personal property, the PG&E stock and the other stock that was sold by December 31, 2019. Mr. Ferguson is to respond by January 7, 2020. The First and Final Report and Account, etc. is continued to January 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and will be deemed under submission on that date. Ms. Klomparens is to provide a proposed order. Mr. Ferguson may also provide an alternate proposed form of order if he wants to.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-10-2019 Continued To Dec-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Advanced From Nov-20-19 To Sep-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Receipt Of The Ex Parte Petition To Increase Bond
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Final Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Aug-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Jennifer Harmon appearing by CourtCall for Richard Kopacz.

The Court stated that it needed the Inventory & Appraisal (I&A) and that an ex parte petition would need to be filed to adjust the bond. Ms. Harmon stated that the I&A was filed and that the probate referee needed to look at the brokerage account, the Putnam account, and the PG&E stock. Ms. Harmon also stated that they filed an ex parte to increase the bond and that she did not have a copy for the Court. Ms. Harmon stated that First Legal said that a copy of the ex parte was delivered to probate and that if the Court does not have a copy that she would have First Legal drop another copy off. The Court inquired as to when the probate referee was provided the information. Ms. Harmon stated that the information was provided to the probate referee at the beginning of this month. The Court stated that the information should have been provided to the probate referee earlier and that it wants either a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve status of administration on file by September 18, 2019. The Court stated that it could only act on a hard copy of the ex parte to increase the bond. A status hearing on receipt of the ex parte petition to increase the bond is set for August 28, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The status on close of estate currently set for November 20, 2019 is advanced to September 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-20-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jenni Harmon appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court noted that the value of the Estate ranges between $100,000.00 and $500,00.00, and stated that it will set bond at $300,000.00. Ms. Harmon stated that the estimated value of the Estate is around $100,000.00, that the statements are old and the banks will not provide any information until someone is appointed, that there is an Individual Retirement Account ("IRA"), that they do not know who the named Beneficiaries are and that there is a tax issue. Ms. Harmon also stated that there is no real property, that the $500,000.00 amount is a mistake and that the petition listed the value of the Estate at $100,000.00. The Court made reference to the Request for Appointment of Probate Referee indicating the $500,000.00 amount that was filed on July 20, 2018 and signed by Robin Klomparens. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submitted. The Court informed Counsel that the bond amount will either be set at $100,000.00 or $300,000.00.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
